title: A dead dogfood repo is skipped silently inside hooks; the warning stays for interactive CLI runs
action:
Per ask-guard-autofix D3. normalizeDogfoodRepos (skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/state.mjs:275-286) console.warns on every dead/unreadable dogfood_repos entry — which fires on EVERY hook invocation and gets rendered as part of unrelated hook errors (it prefixed an AskUserQuestion deny in the triggering screenshot). Change: mark hook context ONCE in the shared entry wrapper hooks/adapter.mjs (e.g. process.env.BEE_HOOK_CONTEXT='1' set before any lib import runs, or an exported flag the libs read — pick what the adapter's structure makes cleanest and least invasive); in normalizeDogfoodRepos, when hook context is marked, SKIP the console.warn (the entry is still skipped, behavior otherwise identical). Interactive CLI runs (status, onboarding — no adapter) keep the warning verbatim. If and only if the adapter already has a fail-open audit-log helper writing .bee/logs/hooks.jsonl, record the skip there; do NOT build new logging machinery.

TESTS: in skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_state.mjs add rows: with hook context marked, a dead entry produces NO warn output and is still skipped; without the marker the warning still fires verbatim. MIRRORS: byte-mirror to .bee/bin/lib/state.mjs and .bee/bin/hooks/adapter.mjs; manifest + ledger parity green.
verify: node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_state.mjs && node hooks/test_write_guard.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && node scripts/ledger_parity.mjs --check
must_haves: a dead dogfood_repos entry inside a hook run prints NOTHING to stderr and is still skipped
the same dead entry in a plain CLI run still warns verbatim
release_manifest --check and ledger_parity --check green at cap
